title: The essence of architectural work - Part 4
excerpt: The cognitive and the humane purpose of architectural work
content_html:
The essence of architectural work - Part 4

In the previous post, we discussed the economic purpose of architecture. The economic purpose is probably the most emphasized facet regarding the Why of architectural work, yet hardest to grasp in its entirety and the hardest to get right. However, this does not mean that the other facets would be of less importance. But as they do not immediately emphasize time and money, they are often pushed aside in our highly commercial environments. Nevertheless, depending on the situation, getting these facets right can have an even bigger impact on time and especially money than the economic facet. Hence, let us discuss these other two facets of the purpose of architectural work in this post:

  • The cognitive purpose
  • The humane purpose

The cognitive purpose of architectural work

At a very basic level, software development is a mapping from a problem domain to a solution domain.

The problem domain consists of all the needs and demands that define and constrain the solution. Many stakeholder groups frame a vast amount of immediate and contextual requirements. This comprises not only the functional requirements but all explicit and implicit requirements that shape the solution. Additionally, the problem domain consists of myriads of facts as well as interconnections and dependencies between them that define the problem domain and in which all the aforementioned requirements are embedded.

Ideally, we would take all these needs and demands and directly translate them into a solution implementation, taking all the encompassing facts and their interrelations into account.

Drowning in problem domain details

The problem is the sheer mass of details on either side of the mapping. We are confronted with thousands, if not millions, of facts, interrelations, requirements, explicit and implicit wishes, demands, whims, and more on the problem domain side. What makes things even more complicated:

  • Many of the demands contradict each other. Sometimes the contradictions stem from different stakeholders. Sometimes, it is even the same person not realizing (or caring) that they created contradicting demands.
  • Many of the demands do not fit into the existing solution design (if a solution already exists). Most people only care about their current demands. They do not care about how their demands fit into an existing solution. They only care that their demands get implemented.
  • Many of the demands are never spoken out. They are just silent expectations that will be held against you later (“It should be obvious that …”) if you fail to elicit them from the respective stakeholders. Good architects develop a sixth sense for these kinds of demands over time.
  • And so on.

It is an art in itself to make all relevant needs, wishes, and demands explicit, resolve the contradiction and organize them in a comprehensible way. Personally, I doubt that AI agents will become good at that because all this has to do with the “messiness” of humans, their egos, and more 1. But this is a different story, I do not intend to discuss here.

Let us simply assume we would be able to gather all the required facts and interrelations, elicit all needs, wishes, and demands from the stakeholders involved, resolve all contradictions, and organize everything in a comprehensible way. These would be many more details than any human can grasp and consider simultaneously while designing and implementing a solution.

Software engineers drown in an abundance of problem domain facts and details.

Drowning in solution domain details

It does not look any better on the solution domain side:

  • We need to understand the details and intricacies of the programming language used.
  • We need to understand the possibilities and intricacies of the language’s ecosystem.
  • We need to understand the frameworks and libraries used.
  • We need to understand the runtime infrastructure, middleware, and environment, its products, tools, and applications.
  • We need to understand the development environment, its options, challenges, and quirks.
  • We need to understand the existing solution: what is put where, how the former facts and requirements were translated into code.
  • We need to understand the APIs we need to connect to, their commands and data structures, and how to access them.
  • And so on.

It is not only that we need to know where all the already implemented facts and requirements are located in the solution. We also need to understand everything else that shapes the solution domain. These are many more details than any human can keep in their head at once. In short:

Software engineers drown in an abundance of solution domain facts and details.

A losing game

Now, let us assume we would try to map all the requirements directly onto the solution with nothing between them. We would need to map a myriad of facts and details on the problem side onto a myriad of facts and details on the solution side. This would result in a mapping so huge, nobody would be able to even cover a tiny fraction of it, let alone the whole mapping.

Whenever we would create a part of the solution, we would forget the majority of needs and demands affecting that part of the solution. We would need to rewrite all parts of our solution over and over again because we would always forget to take relevant needs and demands or other relevant solution details into account. The entire software development process would result in a mess.

Hence, we need a smaller mapping. We need some kind of compression that fits into a human brain and allows us to quickly identify the currently relevant solution parts and to focus on their relevant details. We need a mapping that requires only reduced mental bandwidth, a mapping of a size that does not result in cognitive overload.

Architecture as compression and guidance

That is exactly what architecture provides (or should provide): Architecture basically provides a sort of compressed construction plan we put between all the facts, needs, and demands of the problem domain and the implementation details of the solution domain. It condenses and structures all the facts, needs, and demands (also those pointing into the future) we find in the problem domain, in a way that it provides guidance and orientation when implementing the solution. It reduces the mental bandwidth required to map between problem domain facts and details and solution domain facts and details. Thus, it reduces the likelihood of missing essential demands while implementing the solution.

This also means that an architecture needs to be simple enough to understand it completely, because otherwise it is of little use. If the architecture results in cognitive overload, it does not fulfill its cognitive purpose (a fact still too often neglected by software architects).

Also note that condense and structure includes removing ambiguity from the requirements and prioritizing contradicting demands to enable the implementation of a solution. Ideally, this should not be part of architectural work but be done in business analysis, requirements engineering, or product management. However, based on my experience, more often than not a flood of ambiguous and contradicting demands hits the architects. Thus, be prepared to do the work and do not rely on others doing it.

Unfortunately, most of the discussions regarding software architecture only focus on the provide guidance and orientation part, including lots of suggestions on how to measure compliance with the architecture and the like. Architects tend to think more often in terms of how to control developers than how to support them in doing their work. The best way to ensure that developers adhere to an architecture is to give them something they understand and that makes their lives easier. An architecture only achieves this if we do both parts of the work:

  • Condense and structure the facts, needs, and demands of the problem domain into an architecture
  • Adding guidance and orientation that supports developers in doing their work

We may still add some fitness functions that are measured automatically during CI/CD. But they should aim at providing guidance and orientation, not at controlling developers.

There are many ways to describe such an architecture. Diagrams of the intended system structure and behavior may be part of it (the latter forgotten most of the time). But an architectural description is not limited to diagrams. Guiding principles and constraints are even more important. Structures and flows often evolve and change quickly. But styles tend to be much more durable. Interface descriptions and code skeletons/sketches can also be very useful. Some metrics that help to figure out if a demand is met. And so on.

The core point is that an architecture should provide guidance and reduces cognitive load.

The humane purpose of architectural work

With that, let us move on to the final facet of the Why of architectural work: the humane purpose. This facet is usually neglected, but based on my perception, it can be the most powerful lever of architecture. Thus, let us have a closer look.

Personally, I made the experience that we can often learn a lot from looking into other domains and understanding what they are doing, why, and how. This was one of the reasons why I watched a little anthology called “Abstract” on Netflix. The anthology portrayed designers from many domains. One of the episodes portrayed Ilse Crawford, a famous interior designer. At one place, she said:

“Ultimately, design is a tool to enhance our humanity” – Ilse Crawford

This sentence resonated deeply with me. If we adopt this stance, architecture, being a vital part of software design, ultimately should improve the lives of the people affected by the solutions we create. If you let this sink in for a moment, you will realize how beautiful and powerful this approach is – a very humane one and a lot less technocratic than most other approaches (including my first one). It also encompasses the cognitive purpose we discussed before because it improved the lives of the developers.

You may be tempted to dismiss this perspective as mere idealistic thinking in a cynical world. But before doing so, it is important to understand its vast economic power. Software solutions affect people: the people contributing to them, the people building them, the people running them, the people using them, and other people who have stakes in the solutions.

  • If we improve the lives of the people influencing, building, and running the solutions, we reduce friction and increase the probability of sustainable success. We reduce effort by reducing the amount of discussion and stress. People are happier in general, which is a massively underrated driver of productivity.
  • If we improve the lives of our users, we increase impact. If our users love our solutions, if they feel good, smooth, and frictionless, we will get more users using our solutions more often. I.e., we do not only influence the cost side with our architecture, which has a limited leverage. We influence the revenue side, which has a much bigger lever than the cost side.

Therefore, we should not easily dismiss this humane facet of the purpose of architectural work. Quite the opposite, we should emphasize it a lot more.

However, in this short-sighted, capitalistic world most of us are living in, the predominant cynical stance is that quick money is more important than the well-being of humans. Personally, I love the irony that the cynics deprive themselves of the possibility of making a lot more money because of their short-sighted worldview.

Be aware that following this humane approach ultimately will lead to different priorities regarding the required activities, when and how to carry them out. It may also create a powerful counterbalance to the purely financial perspective of the economic approach discussed in the previous post.

As this facet of architectural work is so massively neglected, there is still a lot to explore. Based on what I have seen, it is more than worth it.

AI and the purpose of architectural work

These days, it is almost impossible to discuss any aspect of software engineering without mentioning AI. And there are a lot of people around who claim that architectural work can be taken over by AI agents. Thus, let us briefly examine this claim in the light of the purpose of architectural work (we will discuss the topic in more depth at the end of this blog series).

Personally, I think that those people never pondered the purpose of architectural work. If you let the three facets we have discussed sink in, it should be clear that the existing AI solutions are far from being able to implement them even remotely. If you feel obliged to contradict, try to make the AI agent of your choice design an architecture that:

  • Minimizes the cumulative costs of a system over its lifetime without compromising the correctness of behavior at runtime
  • Condenses and structures the problem domain and provides guidance and orientation in the solution domain
  • Improves the lives of the people affected

I think you will be out of luck quickly.

This does not mean that every human architect creates architectures that satisfy these criteria. To be frank, most architectures are probably far from it. Still, if we try to satisfy these traits of a good architecture that fulfill the purpose of architectural work, we need humans for it. Humans are not a guarantee for getting a good architecture, but machines are simply not able to navigate all these too human needs, demands, and resulting depths and pitfalls.

On the other hand, this does not mean that AI agents are useless when it comes to architectural work. Quite the opposite. They can support architectural work in many ways. However, they are not able to implement the desired traits of a good architecture on their own.

Always remember:

Ultimately, we create software for humans.

If we forget this, we are in deep trouble – for many reasons.

Reinventing the wheel

When reading the last section, you may want to contradict: But in my project, AI took over architectural work. Of course, you need the expensive frontier models for that. But they are doing it successfully!

The question is: What are those projects doing where AI takes over “architectural work” successfully?

Most of the time, they are reinventing the wheel – a problem I already discussed a while ago in my blog post “Solving the wrong problem. If we look around, we see companies building the millionth instance of an e-commerce, ERP, SCM, CRM, or the like solution. We see them solving the same problems time and again. For the wrong reasons, we failed to create good software components that encapsulate this logic, as we did with technical components like HTTP servers or message brokers. Usually, the fallacy was (and still is) that companies insist on their “uniqueness”, most of the time only nurturing their habits and whims (and thus also disdaining standard software solutions like, e.g., SAP or Salesforce).

I will not delve deeper into the reasoning why we solve the same problems time and again. This might be a discussion for a different post. The point is: We did, we do, and most likely, we will continue doing so. We solve the same kinds of problems time and again. We solved them so often meanwhile that they are deeply ingrained into the training corpus of any LLM.

Still, this is what we do most of the time: solving solved problems. Building the same solution time and again, just with a few insignificant bells and whistles added. If this is what you are doing all the time – well, then of course your AI agent can define your architecture. But then you do not need architectural work. You do not need an architect. You rather need a librarian who pulls the right book from the bookshelf, a book that was written a long time ago, opens it, and starts reading it. This is a job, LLMs are better at than any human.

But again, this is not architectural work. This is just implementing an existing, worn-out blueprint yet another time – a blueprint someone created a long, long time ago.

Interlude

We concluded the discussion of the Why, the purpose of architectural work, by looking at the cognitive and the humane facets of architectural work. It became clear that AI agents are not able to satisfy these traits of architectural work (unless you are solving a solved problem). Still, they can support architectural work in many ways.

In the next post (link will follow), we will move on to the next dimension of architectural work, the What. Stay tuned …

  1. Even if AI agents probably will not become good at understanding human “messiness”, they might still be successful. The reason for this is that humans tend to submit themselves only too willingly to the needs of machines, while they refuse to do the same (or less) if a human asks them for the exact same thing. Hence, it may turn out that the exact same persons will blame themselves for forgetting to make demands explicit while blaming a human for not asking them for it. Personally, I find it very ironic that many humans treat machines better than humans. But this is yet another story. ↩︎

This comprises not only the functional requirements but all explicit and implicit requirements that shape the solution. Additionally, the problem domain consists of myriads of facts as well as interconnections and dependencies between them that define the problem domain and in which all the aforementioned requirements are embedded.

sentences.en: The problem domain consists of all the needs and demands that define and constrain the solution.
sentences.zh: 问题域由定义和约束解决方案的所有需求和要求组成。

sentences.en: Many stakeholder groups frame a vast amount of immediate and contextual requirements.
sentences.zh: 许多利益相关者团体提出了大量直接和背景要求。

sentences.en: This comprises not only the functional requirements but all explicit and implicit requirements that shape the solution.
sentences.zh: 这不仅包括功能需求，还包括塑造解决方案的所有显式和隐式需求。

sentences.en: Additionally, the problem domain consists of myriads of facts as well as interconnections and dependencies between them that define the problem domain and in which all the aforementioned requirements are embedded.
sentences.zh: 此外，问题域由无数的事实以及它们之间的互连和依赖关系组成，这些事实定义了问题域并且嵌入了所有上述需求。

blocks.html_en: Ideally, we would take all these needs and demands and directly translate them into a solution implementation, taking all the encompassing facts and their interrelations into account.

sentences.en: Ideally, we would take all these needs and demands and directly translate them into a solution implementation, taking all the encompassing facts and their interrelations into account.
sentences.zh: 理想情况下，我们将考虑所有这些需求和要求，并将其直接转化为解决方案实施，同时考虑所有事实及其相互关系。

blocks.html_en: Drowning in problem domain details

sentences.en: Drowning in problem domain details
sentences.zh: 淹没在问题领域的细节中

blocks.html_en: The problem is the sheer mass of details on either side of the mapping. We are confronted with thousands, if not millions, of facts, interrelations, requirements, explicit and implicit wishes, demands, whims, and more on the problem domain side. What makes things even more complicated:

sentences.en: The problem is the sheer mass of details on either side of the mapping.
sentences.zh: 问题在于映射两侧的细节数量巨大。

sentences.en: We are confronted with thousands, if not millions, of facts, interrelations, requirements, explicit and implicit wishes, demands, whims, and more on the problem domain side.
sentences.zh: 我们在问题领域面临着数千甚至数百万的事实、相互关系、要求、明确和隐含的愿望、要求、突发奇想等等。

sentences.en: What makes things even more complicated:
sentences.zh: 是什么让事情变得更加复杂：

blocks.html_en: • Many of the demands contradict each other. Sometimes the contradictions stem from different stakeholders. Sometimes, it is even the same person not realizing (or caring) that they created contradicting demands.

sentences.en: Many of the demands contradict each other.
sentences.zh: 许多要求相互矛盾。

sentences.en: Sometimes the contradictions stem from different stakeholders.
sentences.zh: 有时矛盾源于不同的利益相关者。

sentences.en: Sometimes, it is even the same person not realizing (or caring) that they created contradicting demands.
sentences.zh: 有时，甚至是同一个人没有意识到（或关心）他们提出了相互矛盾的要求。

blocks.html_en: • Many of the demands do not fit into the existing solution design (if a solution already exists). Most people only care about their current demands. They do not care about how their demands fit into an existing solution. They only care that their demands get implemented.

sentences.en: Many of the demands do not fit into the existing solution design (if a solution already exists).
sentences.zh: 许多需求不适合现有的解决方案设计（如果解决方案已经存在）。

sentences.en: Most people only care about their current demands.
sentences.zh: 大多数人只关心他们当前的需求。

sentences.en: They do not care about how their demands fit into an existing solution.
sentences.zh: 他们不关心他们的需求如何适应现有的解决方案。

sentences.en: They only care that their demands get implemented.
sentences.zh: 他们只关心自己的要求得到落实。

blocks.html_en: • Many of the demands are never spoken out. They are just silent expectations that will be held against you later (“It should be obvious that …”) if you fail to elicit them from the respective stakeholders. Good architects develop a sixth sense for these kinds of demands over time.

sentences.en: Many of the demands are never spoken out.
sentences.zh: 很多要求从来没有说出来。

sentences.en: They are just silent expectations that will be held against you later (“It should be obvious that …”) if you fail to elicit them from the respective stakeholders.
sentences.zh: 它们只是无声的期望，如果您未能从各自的利益相关者那里引出这些期望，那么稍后这些期望就会对您不利（“显然……”）。

sentences.en: Good architects develop a sixth sense for these kinds of demands over time.
sentences.zh: 随着时间的推移，优秀的建筑师会对这些需求产生第六感。

blocks.html_en: • And so on.

sentences.en: And so on.
sentences.zh: 等等。

blocks.html_en: It is an art in itself to make all relevant needs, wishes, and demands explicit, resolve the contradiction and organize them in a comprehensible way. Personally, I doubt that AI agents will become good at that because all this has to do with the “messiness” of humans, their egos, and more 1. But this is a different story, I do not intend to discuss here.

sentences.en: It is an art in itself to make all relevant needs, wishes, and demands explicit, resolve the contradiction and organize them in a comprehensible way.
sentences.zh: 明确所有相关的需求、愿望和要求，解决矛盾并以可理解的方式组织它们本身就是一门艺术。

sentences.en: Personally, I doubt that AI agents will become good at that because all this has to do with the “messiness” of humans, their egos, and more 1.
sentences.zh: 就我个人而言，我怀疑人工智能代理是否会擅长于此，因为所有这一切都与人类的“混乱”、他们的自我等等有关1。

sentences.en: But this is a different story, I do not intend to discuss here.
sentences.zh: 但这是另外一个故事了，我不打算在这里讨论。

blocks.html_en: Let us simply assume we would be able to gather all the required facts and interrelations, elicit all needs, wishes, and demands from the stakeholders involved, resolve all contradictions, and organize everything in a comprehensible way. These would be many more details than any human can grasp and consider simultaneously while designing and implementing a solution.

sentences.en: Let us simply assume we would be able to gather all the required facts and interrelations, elicit all needs, wishes, and demands from the stakeholders involved, resolve all contradictions, and organize everything in a comprehensible way.
sentences.zh: 让我们简单地假设我们能够收集所有必需的事实和相互关系，引出相关利益相关者的所有需求、愿望和要求，解决所有矛盾，并以可理解的方式组织一切。

sentences.en: These would be many more details than any human can grasp and consider simultaneously while designing and implementing a solution.
sentences.zh: 这些细节比任何人在设计和实施解决方案时能够同时掌握和考虑的细节要多得多。

blocks.html_en: Software engineers drown in an abundance of problem domain facts and details.

sentences.en: Software engineers drown in an abundance of problem domain facts and details.
sentences.zh: 软件工程师淹没在大量的问题领域事实和细节中。

blocks.html_en: Software engineers drown in an abundance of problem domain facts and details.

sentences.en: Software engineers drown in an abundance of problem domain facts and details.
sentences.zh: 软件工程师淹没在大量的问题领域事实和细节中。

blocks.html_en: Drowning in solution domain details

sentences.en: Drowning in solution domain details
sentences.zh: 淹没在解决方案领域的详细信息中

blocks.html_en: It does not look any better on the solution domain side:

sentences.en: It does not look any better on the solution domain side:
sentences.zh: 在解决方案域方面，它看起来并没有更好：

blocks.html_en: • We need to understand the details and intricacies of the programming language used.

sentences.en: We need to understand the details and intricacies of the programming language used.
sentences.zh: 我们需要了解所使用的编程语言的细节和复杂性。

blocks.html_en: • We need to understand the possibilities and intricacies of the language’s ecosystem.

sentences.en: We need to understand the possibilities and intricacies of the language’s ecosystem.
sentences.zh: 我们需要了解语言生态系统的可能性和复杂性。

blocks.html_en: • We need to understand the frameworks and libraries used.

sentences.en: We need to understand the frameworks and libraries used.
sentences.zh: 我们需要了解所使用的框架和库。

blocks.html_en: • We need to understand the runtime infrastructure, middleware, and environment, its products, tools, and applications.

sentences.en: We need to understand the runtime infrastructure, middleware, and environment, its products, tools, and applications.
sentences.zh: 我们需要了解运行时基础设施、中间件和环境、其产品、工具和应用程序。

blocks.html_en: • We need to understand the development environment, its options, challenges, and quirks.

sentences.en: We need to understand the development environment, its options, challenges, and quirks.
sentences.zh: 我们需要了解开发环境、它的选择、挑战和怪癖。

blocks.html_en: • We need to understand the existing solution: what is put where, how the former facts and requirements were translated into code.

sentences.en: We need to understand the existing solution: what is put where, how the former facts and requirements were translated into code.
sentences.zh: 我们需要了解现有的解决方案：什么放在哪里，以前的事实和需求如何转化为代码。

blocks.html_en: • We need to understand the APIs we need to connect to, their commands and data structures, and how to access them.

sentences.en: We need to understand the APIs we need to connect to, their commands and data structures, and how to access them.
sentences.zh: 我们需要了解需要连接的 API、它们的命令和数据结构以及如何访问它们。

blocks.html_en: • And so on.

sentences.en: And so on.
sentences.zh: 等等。

blocks.html_en: It is not only that we need to know where all the already implemented facts and requirements are located in the solution. We also need to understand everything else that shapes the solution domain. These are many more details than any human can keep in their head at once. In short:

sentences.en: It is not only that we need to know where all the already implemented facts and requirements are located in the solution.
sentences.zh: 我们不仅需要知道所有已实施的事实和需求位于解决方案中的何处。

sentences.en: We also need to understand everything else that shapes the solution domain.
sentences.zh: 我们还需要了解塑造解决方案领域的其他一切。

sentences.en: These are many more details than any human can keep in their head at once.
sentences.zh: 这些细节比任何人能一次性记住的要多得多。

sentences.en: In short:
sentences.zh: 简而言之：

blocks.html_en: Software engineers drown in an abundance of solution domain facts and details.

sentences.en: Software engineers drown in an abundance of solution domain facts and details.
sentences.zh: 软件工程师淹没在大量解决方案领域的事实和细节中。

blocks.html_en: Software engineers drown in an abundance of solution domain facts and details.

sentences.en: Software engineers drown in an abundance of solution domain facts and details.
sentences.zh: 软件工程师淹没在大量解决方案领域的事实和细节中。

blocks.html_en: A losing game

sentences.en: A losing game
sentences.zh: 一场失败的比赛

blocks.html_en: Now, let us assume we would try to map all the requirements directly onto the solution with nothing between them. We would need to map a myriad of facts and details on the problem side onto a myriad of facts and details on the solution side. This would result in a mapping so huge, nobody would be able to even cover a tiny fraction of it, let alone the whole mapping.

sentences.en: Now, let us assume we would try to map all the requirements directly onto the solution with nothing between them.
sentences.zh: 现在，让我们假设我们尝试将所有需求直接映射到解决方案上，并且它们之间没有任何关系。

sentences.en: We would need to map a myriad of facts and details on the problem side onto a myriad of facts and details on the solution side.
sentences.zh: 我们需要将问题方面的大量事实和细节映射到解决方案方面的大量事实和细节。

sentences.en: This would result in a mapping so huge, nobody would be able to even cover a tiny fraction of it, let alone the whole mapping.
sentences.zh: 这将导致映射如此之大，没有人能够覆盖其中的一小部分，更不用说整个映射了。

blocks.html_en: Whenever we would create a part of the solution, we would forget the majority of needs and demands affecting that part of the solution. We would need to rewrite all parts of our solution over and over again because we would always forget to take relevant needs and demands or other relevant solution details into account. The entire software development process would result in a mess.

sentences.en: Whenever we would create a part of the solution, we would forget the majority of needs and demands affecting that part of the solution.
sentences.zh: 每当我们创建解决方案的一部分时，我们都会忘记影响该解决方案部分的大多数需求和要求。

sentences.en: We would need to rewrite all parts of our solution over and over again because we would always forget to take relevant needs and demands or other relevant solution details into account.
sentences.zh: 我们需要一遍又一遍地重写解决方案的所有部分，因为我们总是忘记考虑相关的需求和要求或其他相关的解决方案细节。

sentences.en: The entire software development process would result in a mess.
sentences.zh: 整个软件开发过程就会变得一团糟。

blocks.html_en: Hence, we need a smaller mapping. We need some kind of compression that fits into a human brain and allows us to quickly identify the currently relevant solution parts and to focus on their relevant details. We need a mapping that requires only reduced mental bandwidth, a mapping of a size that does not result in cognitive overload.

sentences.en: Hence, we need a smaller mapping.
sentences.zh: 因此，我们需要一个更小的映射。

sentences.en: We need some kind of compression that fits into a human brain and allows us to quickly identify the currently relevant solution parts and to focus on their relevant details.
sentences.zh: 我们需要某种适合人脑的压缩，使我们能够快速识别当前相关的解决方案部分并专注于它们的相关细节。

sentences.en: We need a mapping that requires only reduced mental bandwidth, a mapping of a size that does not result in cognitive overload.
sentences.zh: 我们需要一种只需要减少心理带宽的映射，一种不会导致认知超载的大小的映射。

blocks.html_en: Architecture as compression and guidance

sentences.en: Architecture as compression and guidance
sentences.zh: 建筑作为压缩和引导

blocks.html_en: That is exactly what architecture provides (or should provide): Architecture basically provides a sort of compressed construction plan we put between all the facts, needs, and demands of the problem domain and the implementation details of the solution domain. It condenses and structures all the facts, needs, and demands (also those pointing into the future) we find in the problem domain, in a way that it provides guidance and orientation when implementing the solution. It reduces the mental bandwidth required to map between problem domain facts and details and solution domain facts and details. Thus, it reduces the likelihood of missing essential demands while implementing the solution.

sentences.en: That is exactly what architecture provides (or should provide): Architecture basically provides a sort of compressed construction plan we put between all the facts, needs, and demands of the problem domain and the implementation details of the solution domain.
sentences.zh: 这正是架构所提供（或应该提供）的：架构基本上提供了一种压缩的构建计划，我们将其置于问题域的所有事实、需要和要求与解决方案域的实现细节之间。

sentences.en: It condenses and structures all the facts, needs, and demands (also those pointing into the future) we find in the problem domain, in a way that it provides guidance and orientation when implementing the solution.
sentences.zh: 它浓缩并构建了我们在问题领域中发现的所有事实、需求和需求（以及指向未来的需求），并在实施解决方案时提供指导和方向。

sentences.en: It reduces the mental bandwidth required to map between problem domain facts and details and solution domain facts and details.
sentences.zh: 它减少了在问题域事实和细节与解决方案域事实和细节之间进行映射所需的心理带宽。

sentences.en: Thus, it reduces the likelihood of missing essential demands while implementing the solution.
sentences.zh: 因此，它减少了在实施解决方案时错过基本需求的可能性。

blocks.html_en: This also means that an architecture needs to be simple enough to understand it completely, because otherwise it is of little use. If the architecture results in cognitive overload, it does not fulfill its cognitive purpose (a fact still too often neglected by software architects).

sentences.en: This also means that an architecture needs to be simple enough to understand it completely, because otherwise it is of little use.
sentences.zh: 这也意味着架构需要足够简单才能完全理解它，否则它就没有什么用处。

sentences.en: If the architecture results in cognitive overload, it does not fulfill its cognitive purpose (a fact still too often neglected by software architects).
sentences.zh: 如果架构导致认知过载，它就无法实现其认知目的（这一事实仍然经常被软件架构师忽视）。

blocks.html_en: Also note that condense and structure includes removing ambiguity from the requirements and prioritizing contradicting demands to enable the implementation of a solution. Ideally, this should not be part of architectural work but be done in business analysis, requirements engineering, or product management. However, based on my experience, more often than not a flood of ambiguous and contradicting demands hits the architects. Thus, be prepared to do the work and do not rely on others doing it.

sentences.en: Also note that condense and structure includes removing ambiguity from the requirements and prioritizing contradicting demands to enable the implementation of a solution.
sentences.zh: 另请注意，压缩和结构包括消除需求中的歧义并优先考虑相互矛盾的需求，以实现解决方案。

sentences.en: Ideally, this should not be part of architectural work but be done in business analysis, requirements engineering, or product management.
sentences.zh: 理想情况下，这不应该是架构工作的一部分，而应该在业务分析、需求工程或产品管理中完成。

sentences.en: However, based on my experience, more often than not a flood of ambiguous and contradicting demands hits the architects.
sentences.zh: 然而，根据我的经验，建筑师常常会遇到大量模棱两可和相互矛盾的要求。

sentences.en: Thus, be prepared to do the work and do not rely on others doing it.
sentences.zh: 因此，做好准备去做这项工作，不要依赖别人来做。

blocks.html_en: Unfortunately, most of the discussions regarding software architecture only focus on the provide guidance and orientation part, including lots of suggestions on how to measure compliance with the architecture and the like. Architects tend to think more often in terms of how to control developers than how to support them in doing their work. The best way to ensure that developers adhere to an architecture is to give them something they understand and that makes their lives easier. An architecture only achieves this if we do both parts of the work:

sentences.en: Unfortunately, most of the discussions regarding software architecture only focus on the provide guidance and orientation part, including lots of suggestions on how to measure compliance with the architecture and the like.
sentences.zh: 不幸的是，大多数关于软件架构的讨论只集中在提供指导和定位部分，包括许多关于如何衡量架构合规性的建议等。

sentences.en: Architects tend to think more often in terms of how to control developers than how to support them in doing their work.
sentences.zh: 架构师往往更多地考虑如何控制开发人员，而不是如何支持他们完成工作。

sentences.en: The best way to ensure that developers adhere to an architecture is to give them something they understand and that makes their lives easier.
sentences.zh: 确保开发人员遵守架构的最佳方法是为他们提供他们理解的东西，让他们的生活更轻松。

sentences.en: An architecture only achieves this if we do both parts of the work:
sentences.zh: 只有当我们完成这两部分工作时，架构才能实现这一目标：

blocks.html_en: • Condense and structure the facts, needs, and demands of the problem domain into an architecture

sentences.en: Condense and structure the facts, needs, and demands of the problem domain into an architecture
sentences.zh: 将问题领域的事实、需求和要求压缩并构建为架构

blocks.html_en: • Adding guidance and orientation that supports developers in doing their work

sentences.en: Adding guidance and orientation that supports developers in doing their work
sentences.zh: 添加支持开发人员开展工作的指导和方向

blocks.html_en: We may still add some fitness functions that are measured automatically during CI/CD. But they should aim at providing guidance and orientation, not at controlling developers.

sentences.en: We may still add some fitness functions that are measured automatically during CI/CD.
sentences.zh: 我们仍然可以添加一些在 CI/CD 期间自动测量的适应度函数。

sentences.en: But they should aim at providing guidance and orientation, not at controlling developers.
sentences.zh: 但他们的目标应该是提供指导和方向，而不是控制开发商。

blocks.html_en: There are many ways to describe such an architecture. Diagrams of the intended system structure and behavior may be part of it (the latter forgotten most of the time). But an architectural description is not limited to diagrams. Guiding principles and constraints are even more important. Structures and flows often evolve and change quickly. But styles tend to be much more durable. Interface descriptions and code skeletons/sketches can also be very useful. Some metrics that help to figure out if a demand is met. And so on.

sentences.en: There are many ways to describe such an architecture.
sentences.zh: 有很多方法可以描述这种架构。

sentences.en: Diagrams of the intended system structure and behavior may be part of it (the latter forgotten most of the time).
sentences.zh: 预期系统结构和行为的图表可能是其中的一部分（后者大多数时候被遗忘）。

sentences.en: But an architectural description is not limited to diagrams.
sentences.zh: 但架构描述并不限于图表。

sentences.en: Guiding principles and constraints are even more important.
sentences.zh: 指导原则和约束更为重要。

sentences.en: Structures and flows often evolve and change quickly.
sentences.zh: 结构和流程通常会快速发展和变化。

sentences.en: But styles tend to be much more durable.
sentences.zh: 但款式往往更耐用。

sentences.en: Interface descriptions and code skeletons/sketches can also be very useful.
sentences.zh: 接口描述和代码框架/草图也非常有用。

sentences.en: Some metrics that help to figure out if a demand is met.
sentences.zh: 一些有助于确定需求是否得到满足的指标。

sentences.en: And so on.
sentences.zh: 等等。

blocks.html_en: The core point is that an architecture should provide guidance and reduces cognitive load.

sentences.en: The core point is that an architecture should provide guidance and reduces cognitive load.
sentences.zh: 核心点是架构应该提供指导并减少认知负荷。

blocks.html_en: The humane purpose of architectural work

sentences.en: The humane purpose of architectural work
sentences.zh: 建筑作品的人文目的

blocks.html_en: With that, let us move on to the final facet of the Why of architectural work: the humane purpose. This facet is usually neglected, but based on my perception, it can be the most powerful lever of architecture. Thus, let us have a closer look.

sentences.en: With that, let us move on to the final facet of the Why of architectural work: the humane purpose.
sentences.zh: 说到这里，让我们继续讨论建筑工作的最后一个方面：人性化的目的。

sentences.en: This facet is usually neglected, but based on my perception, it can be the most powerful lever of architecture.
sentences.zh: 这个方面通常被忽视，但根据我的看法，它可能是架构中最强大的杠杆。

sentences.en: Thus, let us have a closer look.
sentences.zh: 因此，让我们仔细看看。

blocks.html_en: Personally, I made the experience that we can often learn a lot from looking into other domains and understanding what they are doing, why, and how. This was one of the reasons why I watched a little anthology called “Abstract” on Netflix. The anthology portrayed designers from many domains. One of the episodes portrayed Ilse Crawford, a famous interior designer. At one place, she said:

sentences.en: Personally, I made the experience that we can often learn a lot from looking into other domains and understanding what they are doing, why, and how.
sentences.zh: 就我个人而言，我的经验是，我们通常可以通过研究其他领域并了解他们在做什么、为什么以及如何做来学到很多东西。

sentences.en: This was one of the reasons why I watched a little anthology called “Abstract” on Netflix.
sentences.zh: 这就是我在 Netflix 上观看一部名为《Abstract》的小选集的原因之一。

sentences.en: The anthology portrayed designers from many domains.
sentences.zh: 该选集描绘了来自多个领域的设计师。

sentences.en: One of the episodes portrayed Ilse Crawford, a famous interior designer.
sentences.zh: 其中一集描绘了著名室内设计师伊尔莎·克劳福德(Ilse Crawford)。

sentences.en: At one place, she said:
sentences.zh: 她在一处说道：

blocks.html_en: “Ultimately, design is a tool to enhance our humanity” – Ilse Crawford

sentences.en: “Ultimately, design is a tool to enhance our humanity” – Ilse Crawford
sentences.zh: “归根结底，设计是增强人性的工具”——Ilse Crawford

blocks.html_en: “Ultimately, design is a tool to enhance our humanity” – Ilse Crawford

sentences.en: “Ultimately, design is a tool to enhance our humanity” – Ilse Crawford
sentences.zh: “归根结底，设计是增强人性的工具”——Ilse Crawford

blocks.html_en: This sentence resonated deeply with me. If we adopt this stance, architecture, being a vital part of software design, ultimately should improve the lives of the people affected by the solutions we create. If you let this sink in for a moment, you will realize how beautiful and powerful this approach is – a very humane one and a lot less technocratic than most other approaches (including my first one). It also encompasses the cognitive purpose we discussed before because it improved the lives of the developers.

sentences.en: This sentence resonated deeply with me.
sentences.zh: 这句话引起了我深深的共鸣。

sentences.en: If we adopt this stance, architecture, being a vital part of software design, ultimately should improve the lives of the people affected by the solutions we create.
sentences.zh: 如果我们采取这种立场，架构作为软件设计的重要组成部分，最终应该改善受我们创建的解决方案影响的人们的生活。

sentences.en: If you let this sink in for a moment, you will realize how beautiful and powerful this approach is – a very humane one and a lot less technocratic than most other approaches (including my first one).
sentences.zh: 如果你稍微理解一下这一点，你就会意识到这种方法是多么美丽和强大——一种非常人性化的方法，并且比大多数其他方法（包括我的第一个方法）少了很多技术官僚。

sentences.en: It also encompasses the cognitive purpose we discussed before because it improved the lives of the developers.
sentences.zh: 它还包含我们之前讨论的认知目的，因为它改善了开发人员的生活。

blocks.html_en: You may be tempted to dismiss this perspective as mere idealistic thinking in a cynical world. But before doing so, it is important to understand its vast economic power. Software solutions affect people: the people contributing to them, the people building them, the people running them, the people using them, and other people who have stakes in the solutions.

sentences.en: You may be tempted to dismiss this perspective as mere idealistic thinking in a cynical world.
sentences.zh: 您可能会想将这种观点视为愤世嫉俗的世界中的理想主义思维而将其驳回。

sentences.en: But before doing so, it is important to understand its vast economic power.
sentences.zh: 但在此之前，了解其巨大的经济实力非常重要。

sentences.en: Software solutions affect people: the people contributing to them, the people building them, the people running them, the people using them, and other people who have stakes in the solutions.
sentences.zh: 软件解决方案影响着人们：为它们做出贡献的人、构建它们的人、运行它们的人、使用它们的人以及与解决方案有利害关系的其他人。

blocks.html_en: • If we improve the lives of the people influencing, building, and running the solutions, we reduce friction and increase the probability of sustainable success. We reduce effort by reducing the amount of discussion and stress. People are happier in general, which is a massively underrated driver of productivity.

sentences.en: If we improve the lives of the people influencing, building, and running the solutions, we reduce friction and increase the probability of sustainable success.
sentences.zh: 如果我们改善影响、构建和运行解决方案的人们的生活，我们就能减少摩擦并增加可持续成功的可能性。

sentences.en: We reduce effort by reducing the amount of discussion and stress.
sentences.zh: 我们通过减少讨论和压力来减少工作量。

sentences.en: People are happier in general, which is a massively underrated driver of productivity.
sentences.zh: 人们总体上更加快乐，这是生产力的一个被严重低估的驱动因素。

blocks.html_en: • If we improve the lives of our users, we increase impact. If our users love our solutions, if they feel good, smooth, and frictionless, we will get more users using our solutions more often. I.e., we do not only influence the cost side with our architecture, which has a limited leverage. We influence the revenue side, which has a much bigger lever than the cost side.

sentences.en: If we improve the lives of our users, we increase impact.
sentences.zh: 如果我们改善用户的生活，我们就会增加影响力。

sentences.en: If our users love our solutions, if they feel good, smooth, and frictionless, we will get more users using our solutions more often.
sentences.zh: 如果我们的用户喜欢我们的解决方案，如果他们感觉良好、流畅且无摩擦，我们将吸引更多用户更频繁地使用我们的解决方案。

sentences.en: I.e., we do not only influence the cost side with our architecture, which has a limited leverage.
sentences.zh: 也就是说，我们的架构不仅会影响成本方面，而架构的影响力有限。

sentences.en: We influence the revenue side, which has a much bigger lever than the cost side.
sentences.zh: 我们影响收入方面，其杠杆比成本方面大得多。

blocks.html_en: Therefore, we should not easily dismiss this humane facet of the purpose of architectural work. Quite the opposite, we should emphasize it a lot more.

sentences.en: Therefore, we should not easily dismiss this humane facet of the purpose of architectural work.
sentences.zh: 因此，我们不应该轻易忽视建筑工作目的的这一人性化方面。

sentences.en: Quite the opposite, we should emphasize it a lot more.
sentences.zh: 恰恰相反，我们应该更加强调这一点。

blocks.html_en: However, in this short-sighted, capitalistic world most of us are living in, the predominant cynical stance is that quick money is more important than the well-being of humans. Personally, I love the irony that the cynics deprive themselves of the possibility of making a lot more money because of their short-sighted worldview.

sentences.en: However, in this short-sighted, capitalistic world most of us are living in, the predominant cynical stance is that quick money is more important than the well-being of humans.
sentences.zh: 然而，在我们大多数人生活的这个短视的资本主义世界中，主流的愤世嫉俗的立场是，快钱比人类的福祉更重要。

sentences.en: Personally, I love the irony that the cynics deprive themselves of the possibility of making a lot more money because of their short-sighted worldview.
sentences.zh: 就我个人而言，我喜欢讽刺的是，愤世嫉俗者由于他们短视的世界观而剥夺了自己赚更多钱的可能性。

blocks.html_en: Be aware that following this humane approach ultimately will lead to different priorities regarding the required activities, when and how to carry them out. It may also create a powerful counterbalance to the purely financial perspective of the economic approach discussed in the previous post.

sentences.en: Be aware that following this humane approach ultimately will lead to different priorities regarding the required activities, when and how to carry them out.
sentences.zh: 请注意，遵循这种人性化的方法最终将导致在所需活动、何时以及如何开展这些活动方面产生不同的优先级。

sentences.en: It may also create a powerful counterbalance to the purely financial perspective of the economic approach discussed in the previous post.
sentences.zh: 它还可能对上一篇文章中讨论的经济方法的纯粹财务视角产生强大的平衡。

blocks.html_en: As this facet of architectural work is so massively neglected, there is still a lot to explore. Based on what I have seen, it is more than worth it.

sentences.en: As this facet of architectural work is so massively neglected, there is still a lot to explore.
sentences.zh: 由于建筑工作的这一方面被严重忽视，因此仍有很多东西需要探索。

sentences.en: Based on what I have seen, it is more than worth it.
sentences.zh: 根据我所看到的，这是非常值得的。

blocks.html_en: AI and the purpose of architectural work

sentences.en: AI and the purpose of architectural work
sentences.zh: 人工智能和建筑工作的目的

blocks.html_en: These days, it is almost impossible to discuss any aspect of software engineering without mentioning AI. And there are a lot of people around who claim that architectural work can be taken over by AI agents. Thus, let us briefly examine this claim in the light of the purpose of architectural work (we will discuss the topic in more depth at the end of this blog series).

sentences.en: These days, it is almost impossible to discuss any aspect of software engineering without mentioning AI.
sentences.zh: 如今，讨论软件工程的任何方面几乎不可能不提及人工智能。

sentences.en: And there are a lot of people around who claim that architectural work can be taken over by AI agents.
sentences.zh: 周围有很多人声称建筑工作可以由人工智能代理接管。

sentences.en: Thus, let us briefly examine this claim in the light of the purpose of architectural work (we will discuss the topic in more depth at the end of this blog series).
sentences.zh: 因此，让我们根据建筑工作的目的简要审视这一主张（我们将在本博客系列的末尾更深入地讨论该主题）。

blocks.html_en: Personally, I think that those people never pondered the purpose of architectural work. If you let the three facets we have discussed sink in, it should be clear that the existing AI solutions are far from being able to implement them even remotely. If you feel obliged to contradict, try to make the AI agent of your choice design an architecture that:

sentences.en: Personally, I think that those people never pondered the purpose of architectural work.
sentences.zh: 就我个人而言，我认为那些人从来没有思考过建筑工作的目的。

sentences.en: If you let the three facets we have discussed sink in, it should be clear that the existing AI solutions are far from being able to implement them even remotely.
sentences.zh: 如果你仔细考虑我们讨论的三个方面，就会发现现有的人工智能解决方案甚至远远无法远程实现它们。

sentences.en: If you feel obliged to contradict, try to make the AI agent of your choice design an architecture that:
sentences.zh: 如果您觉得有必要反驳，请尝试让您选择的人工智能代理设计一个架构：

blocks.html_en: • Minimizes the cumulative costs of a system over its lifetime without compromising the correctness of behavior at runtime

sentences.en: Minimizes the cumulative costs of a system over its lifetime without compromising the correctness of behavior at runtime
sentences.zh: 最大限度地减少系统在其生命周期内的累积成本，而不影响运行时行为的正确性

blocks.html_en: • Condenses and structures the problem domain and provides guidance and orientation in the solution domain

sentences.en: Condenses and structures the problem domain and provides guidance and orientation in the solution domain
sentences.zh: 浓缩和构建问题领域，并在解决方案领域提供指导和方向

blocks.html_en: • Improves the lives of the people affected

sentences.en: Improves the lives of the people affected
sentences.zh: 改善受影响人民的生活

blocks.html_en: I think you will be out of luck quickly.

sentences.en: I think you will be out of luck quickly.
sentences.zh: 我想你很快就会不走运。

blocks.html_en: This does not mean that every human architect creates architectures that satisfy these criteria. To be frank, most architectures are probably far from it. Still, if we try to satisfy these traits of a good architecture that fulfill the purpose of architectural work, we need humans for it. Humans are not a guarantee for getting a good architecture, but machines are simply not able to navigate all these too human needs, demands, and resulting depths and pitfalls.

sentences.en: This does not mean that every human architect creates architectures that satisfy these criteria.
sentences.zh: 这并不意味着每个人类建筑师都会创建满足这些标准的架构。

sentences.en: To be frank, most architectures are probably far from it.
sentences.zh: 坦率地说，大多数架构可能离它还很远。

sentences.en: Still, if we try to satisfy these traits of a good architecture that fulfill the purpose of architectural work, we need humans for it.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，如果我们试图满足良好建筑的这些特征，从而实现建筑工作的目的，我们就需要人类。

sentences.en: Humans are not a guarantee for getting a good architecture, but machines are simply not able to navigate all these too human needs, demands, and resulting depths and pitfalls.
sentences.zh: 人类并不能保证获得良好的架构，但机器根本无法驾驭所有这些过于人类的需求、需求以及由此产生的深度和陷阱。

blocks.html_en: On the other hand, this does not mean that AI agents are useless when it comes to architectural work. Quite the opposite. They can support architectural work in many ways. However, they are not able to implement the desired traits of a good architecture on their own.

sentences.en: On the other hand, this does not mean that AI agents are useless when it comes to architectural work.
sentences.zh: 另一方面，这并不意味着人工智能代理在建筑工作中毫无用处。

sentences.en: Quite the opposite.
sentences.zh: 恰恰相反。

sentences.en: They can support architectural work in many ways.
sentences.zh: 他们可以通过多种方式支持建筑工作。

sentences.en: However, they are not able to implement the desired traits of a good architecture on their own.
sentences.zh: 然而，他们无法独自实现良好架构所需的特征。

blocks.html_en: Always remember:

sentences.en: Always remember:
sentences.zh: 永远记住：

blocks.html_en: Ultimately, we create software for humans.

sentences.en: Ultimately, we create software for humans.
sentences.zh: 最终，我们为人类创建软件。

blocks.html_en: Ultimately, we create software for humans.

sentences.en: Ultimately, we create software for humans.
sentences.zh: 最终，我们为人类创建软件。

blocks.html_en: If we forget this, we are in deep trouble – for many reasons.

sentences.en: If we forget this, we are in deep trouble – for many reasons.
sentences.zh: 如果我们忘记了这一点，我们就会陷入大麻烦——原因有很多。

blocks.html_en: Reinventing the wheel

sentences.en: Reinventing the wheel
sentences.zh: 重新发明轮子

blocks.html_en: When reading the last section, you may want to contradict: But in my project, AI took over architectural work. Of course, you need the expensive frontier models for that. But they are doing it successfully!

sentences.en: When reading the last section, you may want to contradict: But in my project, AI took over architectural work.
sentences.zh: 当阅读最后一节时，你可能会想反驳：但在我的项目中，人工智能接管了建筑工作。

sentences.en: Of course, you need the expensive frontier models for that.
sentences.zh: 当然，你需要昂贵的前沿模型。

sentences.en: But they are doing it successfully!
sentences.zh: 但他们正在成功地做到这一点！

blocks.html_en: The question is: What are those projects doing where AI takes over “architectural work” successfully?

sentences.en: The question is: What are those projects doing where AI takes over “architectural work” successfully?
sentences.zh: 问题是：那些人工智能成功接管“建筑工作”的项目正在做什么？

blocks.html_en: Most of the time, they are reinventing the wheel – a problem I already discussed a while ago in my blog post “Solving the wrong problem. If we look around, we see companies building the millionth instance of an e-commerce, ERP, SCM, CRM, or the like solution. We see them solving the same problems time and again. For the wrong reasons, we failed to create good software components that encapsulate this logic, as we did with technical components like HTTP servers or message brokers. Usually, the fallacy was (and still is) that companies insist on their “uniqueness”, most of the time only nurturing their habits and whims (and thus also disdaining standard software solutions like, e.g., SAP or Salesforce).

sentences.en: Most of the time, they are reinventing the wheel – a problem I already discussed a while ago in my blog post “Solving the wrong problem.
sentences.zh: 大多数时候，他们都在重新发明轮子——这个问题我之前已经在我的博客文章“解决错误的问题”中讨论过。

sentences.en: If we look around, we see companies building the millionth instance of an e-commerce, ERP, SCM, CRM, or the like solution.
sentences.zh: 如果我们环顾四周，我们会看到公司正在构建第一百万个电子商务、ERP、SCM、CRM 或类似解决方案的实例。

sentences.en: We see them solving the same problems time and again.
sentences.zh: 我们看到他们一次又一次地解决同样的问题。

sentences.en: For the wrong reasons, we failed to create good software components that encapsulate this logic, as we did with technical components like HTTP servers or message brokers.
sentences.zh: 由于错误的原因，我们未能创建封装此逻辑的良好软件组件，就像我们对 HTTP 服务器或消息代理等技术组件所做的那样。

sentences.en: Usually, the fallacy was (and still is) that companies insist on their “uniqueness”, most of the time only nurturing their habits and whims (and thus also disdaining standard software solutions like, e.g., SAP or Salesforce).
sentences.zh: 通常，这种谬论过去是（现在仍然是），即公司坚持自己的“独特性”，大多数时候只是培养他们的习惯和突发奇想（因此也鄙视 SAP 或 Salesforce 等标准软件解决方案）。

blocks.html_en: I will not delve deeper into the reasoning why we solve the same problems time and again. This might be a discussion for a different post. The point is: We did, we do, and most likely, we will continue doing so. We solve the same kinds of problems time and again. We solved them so often meanwhile that they are deeply ingrained into the training corpus of any LLM.

sentences.en: I will not delve deeper into the reasoning why we solve the same problems time and again.
sentences.zh: 我不会更深入地探讨为什么我们一次又一次解决同样的问题的原因。

sentences.en: This might be a discussion for a different post.
sentences.zh: 这可能是针对不同帖子的讨论。

sentences.en: The point is: We did, we do, and most likely, we will continue doing so.
sentences.zh: 关键是：我们做到了，我们做到了，而且很可能我们将继续这样做。

sentences.en: We solve the same kinds of problems time and again.
sentences.zh: 我们一次又一次地解决同样类型的问题。

sentences.en: We solved them so often meanwhile that they are deeply ingrained into the training corpus of any LLM.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，我们经常解决这些问题，以至于它们已深深地融入到任何法学硕士的培训语料库中。

blocks.html_en: Still, this is what we do most of the time: solving solved problems. Building the same solution time and again, just with a few insignificant bells and whistles added. If this is what you are doing all the time – well, then of course your AI agent can define your architecture. But then you do not need architectural work. You do not need an architect. You rather need a librarian who pulls the right book from the bookshelf, a book that was written a long time ago, opens it, and starts reading it. This is a job, LLMs are better at than any human.

sentences.en: Still, this is what we do most of the time: solving solved problems.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，这仍然是我们大多数时候所做的：解决已解决的问题。

sentences.en: Building the same solution time and again, just with a few insignificant bells and whistles added.
sentences.zh: 一次又一次地构建相同的解决方案，只是添加了一些无关紧要的花哨的东西。

sentences.en: If this is what you are doing all the time – well, then of course your AI agent can define your architecture.
sentences.zh: 如果这是您一直在做的事情——那么您的人工智能代理当然可以定义您的架构。

sentences.en: But then you do not need architectural work.
sentences.zh: 但这样你就不需要建筑工作了。

sentences.en: You do not need an architect.
sentences.zh: 你不需要建筑师。

sentences.en: You rather need a librarian who pulls the right book from the bookshelf, a book that was written a long time ago, opens it, and starts reading it.
sentences.zh: 你更需要一个图书管理员从书架上拿出合适的书，一本很久以前写的书，打开它，然后开始阅读。

sentences.en: This is a job, LLMs are better at than any human.
sentences.zh: 这是一项法学硕士比任何人都更擅长的工作。

blocks.html_en: But again, this is not architectural work. This is just implementing an existing, worn-out blueprint yet another time – a blueprint someone created a long, long time ago.

sentences.en: But again, this is not architectural work.
sentences.zh: 但再次强调，这不是建筑作品。

sentences.en: This is just implementing an existing, worn-out blueprint yet another time – a blueprint someone created a long, long time ago.
sentences.zh: 这只是又一次实施一个现有的、破旧的蓝图——一个很久很久以前有人创建的蓝图。

blocks.html_en: Interlude

sentences.en: Interlude
sentences.zh: 插曲

blocks.html_en: We concluded the discussion of the Why, the purpose of architectural work, by looking at the cognitive and the humane facets of architectural work. It became clear that AI agents are not able to satisfy these traits of architectural work (unless you are solving a solved problem). Still, they can support architectural work in many ways.

sentences.en: We concluded the discussion of the Why, the purpose of architectural work, by looking at the cognitive and the humane facets of architectural work.
sentences.zh: 我们通过审视建筑工作的认知和人性方面，结束了对建筑工作的原因和目的的讨论。

sentences.en: It became clear that AI agents are not able to satisfy these traits of architectural work (unless you are solving a solved problem).
sentences.zh: 很明显，人工智能代理无法满足建筑工作的这些特征（除非你正在解决一个已解决的问题）。

sentences.en: Still, they can support architectural work in many ways.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，它们仍然可以通过多种方式支持建筑工作。

blocks.html_en: In the next post (link will follow), we will move on to the next dimension of architectural work, the What. Stay tuned …

sentences.en: In the next post (link will follow), we will move on to the next dimension of architectural work, the What.
sentences.zh: 在下一篇文章（链接如下）中，我们将继续讨论建筑工作的下一个维度，即内容。

sentences.en: Stay tuned …
sentences.zh: 敬请关注 …

blocks.html_en: • Even if AI agents probably will not become good at understanding human “messiness”, they might still be successful. The reason for this is that humans tend to submit themselves only too willingly to the needs of machines, while they refuse to do the same (or less) if a human asks them for the exact same thing. Hence, it may turn out that the exact same persons will blame themselves for forgetting to make demands explicit while blaming a human for not asking them for it. Personally, I find it very ironic that many humans treat machines better than humans. But this is yet another story. ↩︎

sentences.en: Even if AI agents probably will not become good at understanding human “messiness”, they might still be successful.
sentences.zh: 即使人工智能代理可能不会擅长理解人类的“混乱”，他们仍然可能会成功。

sentences.en: The reason for this is that humans tend to submit themselves only too willingly to the needs of machines, while they refuse to do the same (or less) if a human asks them for the exact same thing.
sentences.zh: 其原因是，人类往往过于乐意地满足机器的需求，而如果人类要求他们做完全相同的事情，他们就会拒绝做同样的（或更少的）事情。

sentences.en: Hence, it may turn out that the exact same persons will blame themselves for forgetting to make demands explicit while blaming a human for not asking them for it.
sentences.zh: 因此，事实可能是，完全相同的人会责怪自己忘记提出明确的要求，同时责怪人类没有向他们提出要求。

sentences.en: Personally, I find it very ironic that many humans treat machines better than humans.
sentences.zh: 就我个人而言，我觉得非常讽刺的是，许多人对待机器比对待人类更好。

sentences.en: But this is yet another story. ↩︎
sentences.zh: 但这是另一个故事了。 ↩︎

content_html_zh:
建筑作品的本质 - 第 4 部分

在上一篇文章中，我们讨论了建筑的经济目的。 经济目的可能是关于建筑工作的原因中最受强调的方面，但也是最难全面理解和最难正确理解的。 然而，这并不意味着其他方面就不那么重要了。 但由于它们并不立即强调时间和金钱，因此它们经常在我们高度商业化的环境中被推到一边。 然而，根据具体情况，正确处理这些方面可能会对时间（尤其是金钱）产生比经济方面更大的影响。 因此，让我们在这篇文章中讨论建筑工作目的的另外两个方面：

  • 认知目的
  • 人道目的

建筑工作的认知目的

在非常基础的层面上，软件开发是从问题域到解决方案域的映射。

问题域由定义和约束解决方案的所有需求和要求组成。 许多利益相关者团体提出了大量直接和背景要求。 这不仅包括功能需求，还包括塑造解决方案的所有显式和隐式需求。 此外，问题域由无数的事实以及它们之间的互连和依赖关系组成，这些事实定义了问题域并且嵌入了所有上述需求。

理想情况下，我们将考虑所有这些需求和要求，并将其直接转化为解决方案实施，同时考虑所有事实及其相互关系。

淹没在问题领域的细节中

问题在于映射两侧的细节数量巨大。 我们在问题领域面临着数千甚至数百万的事实、相互关系、要求、明确和隐含的愿望、要求、突发奇想等等。 是什么让事情变得更加复杂：

  • 许多要求相互矛盾。 有时矛盾源于不同的利益相关者。 有时，甚至是同一个人没有意识到（或关心）他们提出了相互矛盾的要求。
  • 许多需求不适合现有的解决方案设计（如果解决方案已经存在）。 大多数人只关心他们当前的需求。 他们不关心他们的需求如何适应现有的解决方案。 他们只关心自己的要求得到落实。
  • 很多要求从来没有说出来。 它们只是无声的期望，如果您未能从各自的利益相关者那里引出这些期望，那么稍后这些期望就会对您不利（“显然……”）。 随着时间的推移，优秀的建筑师会对这些需求产生第六感。
  • 等等。

明确所有相关的需求、愿望和要求，解决矛盾并以可理解的方式组织它们本身就是一门艺术。 就我个人而言，我怀疑人工智能代理是否会擅长于此，因为所有这一切都与人类的“混乱”、他们的自我等等有关1。 但这是另外一个故事了，我不打算在这里讨论。

让我们简单地假设我们能够收集所有必需的事实和相互关系，引出相关利益相关者的所有需求、愿望和要求，解决所有矛盾，并以可理解的方式组织一切。 这些细节比任何人在设计和实施解决方案时能够同时掌握和考虑的细节要多得多。

软件工程师淹没在大量的问题领域事实和细节中。

淹没在解决方案领域的详细信息中

在解决方案域方面，它看起来并没有更好：

  • 我们需要了解所使用的编程语言的细节和复杂性。
  • 我们需要了解语言生态系统的可能性和复杂性。
  • 我们需要了解所使用的框架和库。
  • 我们需要了解运行时基础设施、中间件和环境、其产品、工具和应用程序。
  • 我们需要了解开发环境、它的选择、挑战和怪癖。
  • 我们需要了解现有的解决方案：什么放在哪里，以前的事实和需求如何转化为代码。
  • 我们需要了解需要连接的 API、它们的命令和数据结构以及如何访问它们。
  • 等等。

我们不仅需要知道所有已实施的事实和需求位于解决方案中的何处。 我们还需要了解塑造解决方案领域的其他一切。 这些细节比任何人能一次性记住的要多得多。 简而言之：

软件工程师淹没在大量解决方案领域的事实和细节中。

一场失败的比赛

现在，让我们假设我们尝试将所有需求直接映射到解决方案上，并且它们之间没有任何关系。 我们需要将问题方面的大量事实和细节映射到解决方案方面的大量事实和细节。 这将导致映射如此之大，没有人能够覆盖其中的一小部分，更不用说整个映射了。

每当我们创建解决方案的一部分时，我们都会忘记影响该解决方案部分的大多数需求和要求。 我们需要一遍又一遍地重写解决方案的所有部分，因为我们总是忘记考虑相关的需求和要求或其他相关的解决方案细节。 整个软件开发过程就会变得一团糟。

因此，我们需要一个更小的映射。 我们需要某种适合人脑的压缩，使我们能够快速识别当前相关的解决方案部分并专注于它们的相关细节。 我们需要一种只需要减少心理带宽的映射，一种不会导致认知超载的大小的映射。

建筑作为压缩和引导

这正是架构所提供（或应该提供）的：架构基本上提供了一种压缩的构建计划，我们将其置于问题域的所有事实、需要和要求与解决方案域的实现细节之间。 它浓缩并构建了我们在问题领域中发现的所有事实、需求和需求（以及指向未来的需求），并在实施解决方案时提供指导和方向。 它减少了在问题域事实和细节与解决方案域事实和细节之间进行映射所需的心理带宽。 因此，它减少了在实施解决方案时错过基本需求的可能性。

这也意味着架构需要足够简单才能完全理解它，否则它就没有什么用处。 如果架构导致认知过载，它就无法实现其认知目的（这一事实仍然经常被软件架构师忽视）。

另请注意，压缩和结构包括消除需求中的歧义并优先考虑相互矛盾的需求，以实现解决方案。 理想情况下，这不应该是架构工作的一部分，而应该在业务分析、需求工程或产品管理中完成。 然而，根据我的经验，建筑师常常会遇到大量模棱两可和相互矛盾的要求。 因此，做好准备去做这项工作，不要依赖别人来做。

不幸的是，大多数关于软件架构的讨论只集中在提供指导和定位部分，包括许多关于如何衡量架构合规性的建议等。 架构师往往更多地考虑如何控制开发人员，而不是如何支持他们完成工作。 确保开发人员遵守架构的最佳方法是为他们提供他们理解的东西，让他们的生活更轻松。 只有当我们完成这两部分工作时，架构才能实现这一目标：

  • 将问题领域的事实、需求和要求压缩并构建为架构
  • 添加支持开发人员开展工作的指导和方向

我们仍然可以添加一些在 CI/CD 期间自动测量的适应度函数。 但他们的目标应该是提供指导和方向，而不是控制开发商。

有很多方法可以描述这种架构。 预期系统结构和行为的图表可能是其中的一部分（后者大多数时候被遗忘）。 但架构描述并不限于图表。 指导原则和约束更为重要。 结构和流程通常会快速发展和变化。 但款式往往更耐用。 接口描述和代码框架/草图也非常有用。 一些有助于确定需求是否得到满足的指标。 等等。

核心点是架构应该提供指导并减少认知负荷。

建筑作品的人文目的

说到这里，让我们继续讨论建筑工作的最后一个方面：人性化的目的。 这个方面通常被忽视，但根据我的看法，它可能是架构中最强大的杠杆。 因此，让我们仔细看看。

就我个人而言，我的经验是，我们通常可以通过研究其他领域并了解他们在做什么、为什么以及如何做来学到很多东西。 这就是我在 Netflix 上观看一部名为《Abstract》的小选集的原因之一。 该选集描绘了来自多个领域的设计师。 其中一集描绘了著名室内设计师伊尔莎·克劳福德(Ilse Crawford)。 她在一处说道：

“归根结底，设计是增强人性的工具”——Ilse Crawford

这句话引起了我深深的共鸣。 如果我们采取这种立场，架构作为软件设计的重要组成部分，最终应该改善受我们创建的解决方案影响的人们的生活。 如果你稍微理解一下这一点，你就会意识到这种方法是多么美丽和强大——一种非常人性化的方法，并且比大多数其他方法（包括我的第一个方法）少了很多技术官僚。 它还包含我们之前讨论的认知目的，因为它改善了开发人员的生活。

您可能会想将这种观点视为愤世嫉俗的世界中的理想主义思维而将其驳回。 但在此之前，了解其巨大的经济实力非常重要。 软件解决方案影响着人们：为它们做出贡献的人、构建它们的人、运行它们的人、使用它们的人以及与解决方案有利害关系的其他人。

  • 如果我们改善影响、构建和运行解决方案的人们的生活，我们就能减少摩擦并增加可持续成功的可能性。 我们通过减少讨论和压力来减少工作量。 人们总体上更加快乐，这是生产力的一个被严重低估的驱动因素。
  • 如果我们改善用户的生活，我们就会增加影响力。 如果我们的用户喜欢我们的解决方案，如果他们感觉良好、流畅且无摩擦，我们将吸引更多用户更频繁地使用我们的解决方案。 也就是说，我们的架构不仅会影响成本方面，而架构的影响力有限。 我们影响收入方面，其杠杆比成本方面大得多。

因此，我们不应该轻易忽视建筑工作目的的这一人性化方面。 恰恰相反，我们应该更加强调这一点。

然而，在我们大多数人生活的这个短视的资本主义世界中，主流的愤世嫉俗的立场是，快钱比人类的福祉更重要。 就我个人而言，我喜欢讽刺的是，愤世嫉俗者由于他们短视的世界观而剥夺了自己赚更多钱的可能性。

请注意，遵循这种人性化的方法最终将导致在所需活动、何时以及如何开展这些活动方面产生不同的优先级。 它还可能对上一篇文章中讨论的经济方法的纯粹财务视角产生强大的平衡。

由于建筑工作的这一方面被严重忽视，因此仍有很多东西需要探索。 根据我所看到的，这是非常值得的。

人工智能和建筑工作的目的

如今，讨论软件工程的任何方面几乎不可能不提及人工智能。 周围有很多人声称建筑工作可以由人工智能代理接管。 因此，让我们根据建筑工作的目的简要审视这一主张（我们将在本博客系列的末尾更深入地讨论该主题）。

就我个人而言，我认为那些人从来没有思考过建筑工作的目的。 如果你仔细考虑我们讨论的三个方面，就会发现现有的人工智能解决方案甚至远远无法远程实现它们。 如果您觉得有必要反驳，请尝试让您选择的人工智能代理设计一个架构：

  • 最大限度地减少系统在其生命周期内的累积成本，而不影响运行时行为的正确性
  • 浓缩和构建问题领域，并在解决方案领域提供指导和方向
  • 改善受影响人民的生活

我想你很快就会不走运。

这并不意味着每个人类建筑师都会创建满足这些标准的架构。 坦率地说，大多数架构可能离它还很远。 尽管如此，如果我们试图满足良好建筑的这些特征，从而实现建筑工作的目的，我们就需要人类。 人类并不能保证获得良好的架构，但机器根本无法驾驭所有这些过于人类的需求、需求以及由此产生的深度和陷阱。

另一方面，这并不意味着人工智能代理在建筑工作中毫无用处。 恰恰相反。 他们可以通过多种方式支持建筑工作。 然而，他们无法独自实现良好架构所需的特征。

永远记住：

最终，我们为人类创建软件。

如果我们忘记了这一点，我们就会陷入大麻烦——原因有很多。

重新发明轮子

当阅读最后一节时，你可能会想反驳：但在我的项目中，人工智能接管了建筑工作。 当然，你需要昂贵的前沿模型。 但他们正在成功地做到这一点！

问题是：那些人工智能成功接管“建筑工作”的项目正在做什么？

大多数时候，他们都在重新发明轮子——这个问题我之前已经在我的博客文章“解决错误的问题”中讨论过。 如果我们环顾四周，我们会看到公司正在构建第一百万个电子商务、ERP、SCM、CRM 或类似解决方案的实例。 我们看到他们一次又一次地解决同样的问题。 由于错误的原因，我们未能创建封装此逻辑的良好软件组件，就像我们对 HTTP 服务器或消息代理等技术组件所做的那样。 通常，这种谬论过去是（现在仍然是），即公司坚持自己的“独特性”，大多数时候只是培养他们的习惯和突发奇想（因此也鄙视 SAP 或 Salesforce 等标准软件解决方案）。

我不会更深入地探讨为什么我们一次又一次解决同样的问题的原因。 这可能是针对不同帖子的讨论。 关键是：我们做到了，我们做到了，而且很可能我们将继续这样做。 我们一次又一次地解决同样类型的问题。 与此同时，我们经常解决这些问题，以至于它们已深深地融入到任何法学硕士的培训语料库中。

尽管如此，这仍然是我们大多数时候所做的：解决已解决的问题。 一次又一次地构建相同的解决方案，只是添加了一些无关紧要的花哨的东西。 如果这是您一直在做的事情——那么您的人工智能代理当然可以定义您的架构。 但这样你就不需要建筑工作了。 你不需要建筑师。 你更需要一个图书管理员从书架上拿出合适的书，一本很久以前写的书，打开它，然后开始阅读。 这是一项法学硕士比任何人都更擅长的工作。

但再次强调，这不是建筑作品。 这只是又一次实施一个现有的、破旧的蓝图——一个很久很久以前有人创建的蓝图。

插曲

我们通过审视建筑工作的认知和人性方面，结束了对建筑工作的原因和目的的讨论。 很明显，人工智能代理无法满足建筑工作的这些特征（除非你正在解决一个已解决的问题）。 尽管如此，它们仍然可以通过多种方式支持建筑工作。

在下一篇文章（链接如下）中，我们将继续讨论建筑工作的下一个维度，即内容。 敬请关注 …

  1. 即使人工智能代理可能不会擅长理解人类的“混乱”，他们仍然可能会成功。 其原因是，人类往往过于乐意地满足机器的需求，而如果人类要求他们做完全相同的事情，他们就会拒绝做同样的（或更少的）事情。 因此，事实可能是，完全相同的人会责怪自己忘记提出明确的要求，同时责怪人类没有向他们提出要求。 就我个人而言，我觉得非常讽刺的是，许多人对待机器比对待人类更好。 但这是另一个故事了。 ↩︎
